files.patch:
@@ -105,8 +105,8 @@
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","title":"Root-cause and fix mux-server retention and leak classes across pane tab window and session lifecycles","description":"# Goal
Eliminate the mux-server leak and retention classes that undermine the original project mission.

This task exists because the user’s motivating complaint specifically called out mux-server memory leaks. Those leaks are likely to live in lifecycle ownership, registry maps, sessionhandler state, callback or subscription cleanup, detach or reconnect behavior, and shutdown paths.","design":"# Design
1. Use the instrumentation from the preceding bead to isolate concrete mux-server leak classes.
2. Fix lifecycle ownership and cleanup bugs around pane, tab, window, domain, and session state.
3. Pay special attention to reconnect, detach, callback, and subscription cleanup semantics so old state cannot remain reachable forever.
4. Add focused regression coverage that proves the specific retention class is gone.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Confirmed mux-server leak classes covered by this bead have root-cause fixes.
2. Cleanup semantics for the affected lifecycle paths are explicit and test-covered.
3. Instrumentation shows the relevant counts and memory signals return to expected baselines after churn.
4. Completion evidence names the exact leak classes fixed instead of claiming generic improvement.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This bead is the direct answer to the original mux leak complaint. It should not be allowed to close on broad hand-waving.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:29.684813Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-11T01:44:17.587843Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T01:44:17.586607Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","leaks","mux","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:04.371299Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:39.293064Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:39.526782Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:29.684813Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:39.073260Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.3","title":"Root-cause and fix runtime, scrollback, capture, search, and file-descriptor retention outside the mux server","description":"# Goal
Eliminate the major non-mux retention and pressure sources that can still sink long-haul agent workloads.

This task exists because even after mux-server leaks are fixed, the system can still fail its mission through scrollback growth, capture backlogs, search caches, descriptor leaks, or long-lived runtime data structures that never release pressure.","design":"# Design
1. Use the instrumentation layer to isolate pressure and retention sources outside the mux server itself.
2. Prioritize scrollback, capture, search, and descriptor lifecycle behavior because those are directly tied to multi-agent workload scale.
3. Fix ownership and reclamation bugs rather than papering over them with ad hoc trimming.
4. Add targeted regression and benchmark evidence for each important fix.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Confirmed runtime-side retention or descriptor classes covered by this bead have root-cause fixes.
2. The fixes are backed by regression coverage and evidence that the relevant counters return to baseline after stress.
3. The resulting behavior is stable enough to support long-haul soak without hidden pressure growth.
4. Completion evidence distinguishes runtime-side fixes from mux-server-side fixes.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"Implemented immediate runtime pane-state compaction in discovery + maintenance and search watermark pruning for disappeared panes. Added unit regressions in crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs and crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/indexing_pipeline.rs, plus proof harness tests/e2e/test_ft_xbnl0_4_3_runtime_retention.sh with artifacts under tests/e2e/artifacts/goal-line/ft-xbnl0.4.3/runtime_retention/20260419T154821Z. Exact requested validation command failed on worker image because /opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang is absent (requested_worker_test.log). Worker-compatible cargo test failed in unrelated pre-existing vendored_async_contract_behavioral compile errors (worker_compatible_test.log, E0277/E0282 at lines 2182/2334).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:30.095566Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T16:07:26.419069Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T16:07:26.418769Z","close_reason":"Implemented runtime/search retention compaction; recorded validation artifacts and unrelated worker/test blockers","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fd","goal-line","performance","reliability","scrollback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:04.627822Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:40.023729Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:40.257320Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:30.095566Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:39.764566Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.4","title":"Add deterministic leak-oracle regressions for churn, reconnect, shutdown, restore, and workflow storms","description":"# Goal
Turn the leak and lifecycle fixes into deterministic regression tests that fail before future contributors reintroduce the same classes of breakage.

This task exists because long-haul issues are expensive to rediscover by soak alone. The finish-line program needs deterministic tests that model the dangerous lifecycle patterns directly.","design":"# Design
1. Build targeted churn scenarios for pane creation and teardown, reconnect, detach, shutdown, restore, and workflow-storm style activity.
2. Use deterministic runtime or focused harnesses so the results are reproducible.
3. Assert on leak-oracle signals from the instrumentation layer rather than only on functional output.
4. Keep the scenarios close enough to real workloads that later soak work benefits from the same invariants.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Covered lifecycle regressions have deterministic tests that fail when retention or cleanup behavior regresses.
2. The tests assert on relevant counters, ownership signals, or observable cleanup outcomes.
3. The suite is repeatable and suitable for regular remote verification.
4. Completion evidence records representative test runs and artifacts.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"Soak can tell us the system got sick. This bead is what makes the common failure classes cheap to catch earlier.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:30.541457Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T16:46:12.484873Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T16:46:12.484676Z","close_reason":"Deterministic leak-oracle regressions added; evidence at tests/e2e/artifacts/goal-line/ft-xbnl0.4.4/leak_oracle_regressions/20260419T163650Z (remote run blocked by pre-existing runtime_compat tokio::select import failure before new tests executed)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","oracles","reliability","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:04.891706Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:30.541457Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:40.498175Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:40.784385Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","title":"Build the long-haul 50, 100, and 200-pane swarm soak harness and benchmark matrix","description":"# Goal
Create the workload harness that proves FrankenTerm can actually handle the large multi-agent operating model it was built for.

This task exists because the finish line is not satisfied by unit tests and short integration runs. The project needs a representative long-haul harness that exercises the control plane, mux, scrollback, search, and operator paths under realistic swarm pressure.","design":"# Design
1. Define representative 50, 100, and 200-pane workload profiles, including idle periods, bursty output, reconnects, search, capture, and policy-sensitive actions.
2. Build a soak harness that supports both shorter developer-smoke runs and release-scale long-haul runs so the workload definitions get exercised regularly instead of only at release time.
3. Record benchmark-style metrics and artifacts during the soak so later gates can reason about trend lines rather than a single pass fail.
4. Keep the harness operator-readable, segmentable, and repeatable enough for future release cycles and incident follow-up.","acceptance_criteria":"1. A repeatable soak harness exists for representative multi-agent workloads at 50, 100, and 200 pane scale.
2. The harness records enough metrics and artifacts to diagnose failures without local guesswork.
3. The harness is tied to concrete performance and stability expectations rather than being a best-effort stress script.
4. Completion evidence includes at least one representative run record and artifact bundle layout.
5. The harness includes a shorter smoke profile and a release-scale long-haul profile so the same workload model is usable in both frequent validation and deep proof runs.
6. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This is the bead that transforms the user’s original mission statement into an executable test campaign.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:30.976172Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T16:46:34.837113Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","performance","reliability","soak"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:05.148984Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:07.506745Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:41.420840Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:41.729476Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:30.976172Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:41.086328Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","title":"Turn runtime SLOs, soak confidence, and performance budgets into hard release gates","description":"# Goal
Convert reliability and performance evidence into explicit release gates.

This task exists because data that is not attached to a gate will be ignored the next time the schedule gets tight. The finish-line story needs hard go or no-go criteria for stability, leak behavior, and performance budgets.","design":"# Design
1. Define the SLO and budget thresholds that matter for the finish line.
2. Connect those thresholds to the instrumentation, deterministic regressions, and soak outputs from earlier beads.
3. Make failure modes explicit and actionable so the gates are useful rather than ceremonial.
4. Record the resulting gate definitions in bead-native form for release work.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Reliability and performance budgets are explicit and machine-checkable or procedure-checkable.
2. The release process has named gates for leak behavior, soak confidence, and relevant performance metrics.
3. Gate failures produce actionable diagnostics.
4. Release-readiness tasks can rely on these gates rather than reinventing them.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This bead is what prevents multi-day swarm reliability from devolving back into a vibes-based claim.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:31.417607Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-03T17:44:49.327598Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","release-readiness","reliability","slo"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:41.955060Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:07.766069Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:31.417607Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:42.385704Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5","title":"[EPIC] Release readiness, operator polish, and permanent regression guards","description":"# Goal
Turn the completed technical work into a ship-ready, operator-trustworthy project: final docs, guards, tuning guidance, end-to-end acceptance scenarios, and a closure bundle that future contributors can rely on.

This epic exists because a project can be technically improved and still not be finish-line ready if docs lie, CI does not defend the invariants, or no one has captured the evidence and workflows that define ready for real multi-agent use.","design":"# Design
1. Rewrite the narrative around the final native architecture and honest supported behavior.
2. Add permanent machine checks for the invariants this program establishes.
3. Expose live health and incident-diagnostics surfaces so operators can actually use the reliability work under pressure.
4. Polish first-run setup, environment validation, and recovery entrypoints so the finished system is operable from the first real interaction.
5. Validate the original mission workflows end to end, not just unit by unit.
6. Package the result into a final closure bundle with explicit remaining risks, if any.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Docs and operator guidance describe the final system rather than a migration-in-progress story.
2. CI, doctor, and audit surfaces fail fast when `tokio`, `smol`, or fake supported capabilities creep back in.
3. Operator acceptance scenarios covering the original mission are executed with artifacts.
4. A final release-candidate checklist and evidence bundle exist in bead-native form.
5. Operator-facing health and diagnostic workflows are first-class outputs of this epic, not incidental notes.
6. First-run setup and recovery entrypoints are polished enough to be part of the supported operator story rather than an undocumented initiation ritual.","notes":"This epic is what prevents the repo from sliding back into technically interesting but operationally ambiguous. It closes the loop between implementation, proof, and operator trust.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:52.840501Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:09:15.048719Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","docs","goal-line","operator","release-readiness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:52.840501Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","title":"Rewrite README, AGENTS, and operator docs around the final native Asupersync architecture and honest support matrix","description":"# Goal
Make the repository narrative match the finished system.

This task exists because docs that still describe migration debt, placeholder capabilities, or outdated runtime assumptions will immediately undermine the credibility of the finish-line push.","design":"# Design
1. Rewrite the high-level architecture story around the final native Asupersync model.
2. Update AGENTS guidance and operator docs to reflect the real supported capability matrix, verification expectations, and live health or diagnostic surfaces added by this program.
3. Remove or rewrite stale references to transitional architecture that no longer exists.
4. Keep the docs specific enough that future agents can work from them without reopening old planning documents.","acceptance_criteria":"1. README, AGENTS, and core operator docs describe the final system rather than a migration in progress.
2. Runtime, support-matrix, and verification language is accurate and current.
3. Operators and future agents can understand the intended architecture and supported surfaces directly from the docs.
4. The updated docs are cross-linked to the finish-line evidence model where appropriate.
5. Documentation examples, support claims, and operator procedures are validated against the shipped commands, recorded verification artifacts, live diagnostic surfaces, and the first-run or recovery path so the docs cannot drift ahead of reality.","notes":"This bead is the documentation counterpart to the code cleanup. If the narrative stays stale, the project will feel unfinished even after the code improves.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:31.852785Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:16.414627Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","docs","goal-line","operator"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:05.535955Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:42.715747Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:43.022385Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:43.471912Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:31.852785Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:09.776559Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:14.259339Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","title":"Add permanent CI, doctor, and audit guards for zero tokio smol and zero fake supported capabilities","description":"# Goal
Make the finish-line invariants self-defending.

This task exists because the project needs durable protection against two classes of regression: runtime debt creeping back in and fake supported capabilities quietly reappearing.","design":"# Design
1. Add or extend CI checks, doctor surfaces, and audit scripts that defend the no-Tokio no-Smol property and the honest support-matrix property.
2. Keep the checks actionable so failures explain what violated the rule and where.
3. Wire the checks into the normal contributor path instead of leaving them as optional extras.
4. Reuse the evidence and inventories built earlier in the program where possible.
5. Validate not only code invariants but also the verification contract itself where practical, including required artifact manifests, log schema, and deterministic E2E entrypoints.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The key finish-line invariants are defended by permanent automated or semi-automated checks.
2. Contributors can run the relevant checks locally and in CI.
3. Violations are specific enough to fix quickly.
4. The release lane can trust these guards as part of ongoing maintenance.
5. Selected deterministic E2E scripts, artifact manifests, and structured-log expectations are automatically checked or linted so the testing and evidence discipline in this program does not silently decay.
6. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This bead is how the finish-line work stays finished after the first release candidate.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyTiger","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:32.309029Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T16:48:43.341734Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T16:48:43.341498Z","close_reason":"finish-line guards passing; artifact docs/ft-xbnl0-5-2-finish-line-guards-validation.json","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","ci","doctor","goal-line","quality-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:05.793483Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:43.811867Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:44.145075Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:32.309029Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+  "last_updated": "2026-04-19",
+  "description": "Blocking release-readiness policy for the ft-xbnl0 finish line. The evaluator in scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_4_6_release_gates.sh consumes this file to turn leak evidence, permanent guards, soak confidence, and runtime performance budgets into stable go/no-go checks.",

@@ -0,0 +1,425 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# ft-xbnl0.4.6 — release-readiness gate evaluator.
+set -euo pipefail
+
+ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
+POLICY_PATH="${ROOT_DIR}/docs/ft-xbnl0-4-6-release-gates.json"
+OUT_PATH="${ROOT_DIR}/docs/ft-xbnl0-4-6-release-gates-validation.json"
+SELF_TEST=0
+
+usage() {
+  cat <<'USAGE'
+Usage: check_ft_xbnl0_4_6_release_gates.sh [options]
+
+Options:
+  --root <path>          Override repository root
+  --policy-path <path>   Override gate policy JSON path
+  --output <path>        Output report JSON path
+  --self-test            Run built-in evaluator self-tests
+  -h, --help             Show this help
+
+Exit codes:
+  0  release gates passed
+  1  release gates blocked or policy malformed
+  2  evaluator internal error
+USAGE
+}
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+  case "$1" in
+    --root) ROOT_DIR="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+    --policy-path) POLICY_PATH="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+    --output) OUT_PATH="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+    --self-test) SELF_TEST=1; shift ;;
+    -h|--help) usage; exit 0 ;;
+    *) echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2; usage >&2; exit 2 ;;
+  esac
+done
+
+mkdir -p "$(dirname "${OUT_PATH}")"
+
+python3 - "${ROOT_DIR}" "${POLICY_PATH}" "${OUT_PATH}" "${SELF_TEST}" <<'PY'
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import copy
+import json
+import sys
+import tempfile
+from dataclasses import dataclass
+from datetime import datetime, timezone
+from pathlib import Path
+from typing import Any
+
+
+@dataclass
+class GateFailure(Exception):
+    code: str
+    message: str
+    detail: dict[str, Any] | None = None
+
+
+def now_iso() -> str:
+    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
+
+
+def fail(code: str, message: str, detail: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> None:
+    raise GateFailure(code=code, message=message, detail=detail)
+
+
+def require(cond: bool, code: str, message: str, detail: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> None:
+    if not cond:
+        fail(code, message, detail)
+
+
+def load_json(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
+
+
+def newest_summary(root: Path) -> Path | None:
+    if not root.exists():
+        return None
+    candidates = sorted(root.glob("*/summary.json"))
+    return candidates[-1] if candidates else None
+
+
+def evaluate_policy(root: Path, policy_path: Path, policy: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    require(
+        policy.get("contract_id") == "ft.xbnl0.4.6.release_gates.v1",
+        "invalid_contract_id",
+        "unexpected contract_id",
+    )
+    thresholds = policy.get("thresholds", {})
+    required_pane_scales = thresholds.get("required_pane_scales")
+    require(isinstance(required_pane_scales, list) and required_pane_scales,
+            "missing_required_pane_scales",
+            "thresholds.required_pane_scales must be a non-empty list")
+    required_metric_count = thresholds.get("required_metric_count")
+    min_smoke_cycles = thresholds.get("min_smoke_cycles")
+    min_release_cycles = thresholds.get("min_release_cycles")
+    max_peak_rss_mb = thresholds.get("max_peak_rss_mb")
+    max_duration_s = thresholds.get("max_duration_s")
+    required_backpressure_tiers = thresholds.get("required_backpressure_tiers", [])
+
+    artifact_roots = policy.get("artifact_roots", {})
+    leak_root = root / artifact_roots["leak_oracle_root"]
+    soak_root = root / artifact_roots["soak_matrix_root"]
+    guard_report_path = root / artifact_roots["guard_report"]
+
+    checks: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
+
+    leak_summary_path = newest_summary(leak_root)
+    leak_detail = {
+        "artifact_root": leak_root.as_posix(),
+        "summary_path": None if leak_summary_path is None else leak_summary_path.as_posix(),
+    }
+    if leak_summary_path is None:
+        checks.append({
+            "gate_id": "REL-01-leak-oracle",
+            "name": "Leak behavior",
+            "status": "failed",
+            "reason_code": "leak_gate_missing_summary",
+            "blocking": True,
+            "detail": leak_detail | {
+                "action": "Run the ft-xbnl0.4.4 leak-oracle harness and capture a passing summary.json bundle."
+            },
+        })
+    else:
+        leak_summary = load_json(leak_summary_path)
+        leak_passed = leak_summary.get("status") == "passed"
+        checks.append({
+            "gate_id": "REL-01-leak-oracle",
+            "name": "Leak behavior",
+            "status": "passed" if leak_passed else "failed",
+            "reason_code": "leak_gate_passed" if leak_passed else "leak_gate_failed_summary",
+            "blocking": True,
+            "detail": leak_detail | {
+                "summary_status": leak_summary.get("status"),
+                "action": "Fix the failing deterministic leak regression or rerun the lane until status=passed.",
+            },
+        })
+
+    if not guard_report_path.exists():
+        checks.append({
+            "gate_id": "REL-02-guard-surface",
+            "name": "Permanent guard surface",
+            "status": "failed",
+            "reason_code": "guard_report_missing",
+            "blocking": True,
+            "detail": {
+                "guard_report": guard_report_path.as_posix(),
+                "action": "Generate docs/ft-xbnl0-5-2-finish-line-guards-validation.json before trusting release-readiness."
+            },
+        })
+    else:
+        guard_report = load_json(guard_report_path)
+        guard_passed = guard_report.get("status") == "passed"
+        checks.append({
+            "gate_id": "REL-02-guard-surface",
+            "name": "Permanent guard surface",
+            "status": "passed" if guard_passed else "failed",
+            "reason_code": "guard_surface_passed" if guard_passed else "guard_surface_failed",
+            "blocking": True,
+            "detail": {
+                "guard_report": guard_report_path.as_posix(),
+                "guard_status": guard_report.get("status"),
+                "action": "Repair the permanent finish-line guards before using the release decision."
+            },
+        })
+
+    soak_wrapper_path = newest_summary(soak_root)
+    if soak_wrapper_path is None:
+        checks.append({
+            "gate_id": "REL-03-soak-confidence",
+            "name": "Soak confidence",
+            "status": "failed",
+            "reason_code": "soak_wrapper_missing",
+            "blocking": True,
+            "detail": {
+                "artifact_root": soak_root.as_posix(),
+                "action": "Run the ft-xbnl0.4.5 soak wrapper and save a wrapper summary.json."
+            },
+        })
+        checks.append({
+            "gate_id": "REL-04-performance-budget",
+            "name": "Performance budget",
+            "status": "failed",
+            "reason_code": "performance_source_missing",
+            "blocking": True,
+            "detail": {
+                "artifact_root": soak_root.as_posix(),
+                "action": "Run the ft-xbnl0.4.5 soak matrix so explicit RSS/duration budgets can be checked."
+            },
+        })
+    else:
+        wrapper = load_json(soak_wrapper_path)
+        smoke_cycles = int(wrapper.get("profiles", {}).get("smoke", {}).get("cycles", 0))
+        release_summaries = wrapper.get("profiles", {}).get("release", {}).get("summaries", [])
+        release_cycles = len(release_summaries)
+        smoke_summary_path = Path(wrapper.get("profiles", {}).get("smoke", {}).get("summary", ""))
+        all_summary_paths = [smoke_summary_path] + [Path(path) for path in release_summaries]
+
+        summaries: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
+        missing_nested = []
+        for summary_path in all_summary_paths:
+            if not summary_path or not summary_path.exists():
+                missing_nested.append(summary_path.as_posix())
+                continue
+            summaries.append(load_json(summary_path))
+
+        pane_scales = sorted({
+            int(scale)
+            for summary in summaries
+            for scale in summary.get("pane_scales", [])
+        })
+        metric_counts = sorted({int(summary.get("tests_run", 0)) for summary in summaries})
+        release_shapes = sorted({
+            json.dumps({
+                "tests_run": summary.get("tests_run"),
+                "pane_scales": summary.get("pane_scales"),
+                "metric_names": summary.get("metric_names"),
+            }, sort_keys=True)
+            for summary in summaries[1:]
+        })
+        release_consistent = len(release_shapes) == 1 if summaries[1:] else False
+        peak_rss_mb = max((float(summary.get("peak_rss_mb", 0.0)) for summary in summaries), default=0.0)
+        max_duration = max((float(summary.get("max_duration_s", 0.0)) for summary in summaries), default=0.0)
+        highest_tiers = sorted({
+            summary.get("highest_backpressure_tier")
+            for summary in summaries
+            if summary.get("highest_backpressure_tier") is not None
+        })
+        backpressure_ok = any(tier in required_backpressure_tiers for tier in highest_tiers)
+
+        soak_passed = (
+            wrapper.get("status") == "passed"
+            and smoke_cycles >= min_smoke_cycles
+            and release_cycles >= min_release_cycles
+            and not missing_nested
+            and pane_scales == required_pane_scales
+            and release_consistent
+        )
+        checks.append({
+            "gate_id": "REL-03-soak-confidence",
+            "name": "Soak confidence",
+            "status": "passed" if soak_passed else "failed",
+            "reason_code": "soak_confidence_passed" if soak_passed else "soak_confidence_failed",
+            "blocking": True,
+            "detail": {
+                "wrapper_summary": soak_wrapper_path.as_posix(),
+                "wrapper_status": wrapper.get("status"),
+                "smoke_cycles": smoke_cycles,
+                "release_cycles": release_cycles,
+                "missing_nested_summaries": missing_nested,
+                "pane_scales": pane_scales,
+                "release_consistent": release_consistent,
+                "action": "Regenerate the smoke/release soak matrix until the wrapper summary is passed and the nested summaries agree.",
+            },
+        })
+
+        performance_passed = (
+            not missing_nested
+            and wrapper.get("status") == "passed"
+            and metric_counts == [required_metric_count]
+            and peak_rss_mb <= max_peak_rss_mb
+            and max_duration <= max_duration_s
+            and backpressure_ok
+        )
+        checks.append({
+            "gate_id": "REL-04-performance-budget",
+            "name": "Performance budget",
+            "status": "passed" if performance_passed else "failed",
+            "reason_code": "performance_budget_passed" if performance_passed else "performance_budget_failed",
+            "blocking": True,
+            "detail": {
+                "wrapper_summary": soak_wrapper_path.as_posix(),
+                "metric_counts": metric_counts,
+                "peak_rss_mb": peak_rss_mb,
+                "max_duration_s": max_duration,
+                "highest_backpressure_tiers": highest_tiers,
+                "required_metric_count": required_metric_count,
+                "max_peak_rss_mb": max_peak_rss_mb,
+                "max_duration_s_budget": max_duration_s,
+                "required_backpressure_tiers": required_backpressure_tiers,
+                "action": "Reduce cost or tune the runtime/workload until RSS, duration, and backpressure expectations hold.",
+            },
+        })
+
+    overall_status = "passed"
+    if any(check["blocking"] and check["status"] != "passed" for check in checks):
+        overall_status = "failed"
+
+    return {
+        "checked_at": now_iso(),
+        "policy_path": policy_path.as_posix(),
+        "repo_root": root.as_posix(),
+        "status": overall_status,
+        "checks": checks,
+        "summary": {
+            "blocking_failed": sum(1 for check in checks if check["blocking"] and check["status"] != "passed"),
+            "latest_leak_summary": None if leak_summary_path is None else leak_summary_path.as_posix(),
+            "latest_soak_wrapper_summary": None if soak_wrapper_path is None else soak_wrapper_path.as_posix(),
+            "guard_report": guard_report_path.as_posix(),
+        },
+    }
+
+
+def run_self_tests() -> None:
+    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
+        root = Path(td)
+        policy = {
+            "contract_id": "ft.xbnl0.4.6.release_gates.v1",
+            "artifact_roots": {
+                "leak_oracle_root": "leak",
+                "soak_matrix_root": "soak",
+                "guard_report": "guards.json",
+            },
+            "thresholds": {
+                "required_pane_scales": [1, 50, 100, 200],
+                "required_metric_count": 8,
+                "min_smoke_cycles": 1,
+                "min_release_cycles": 3,
+                "max_peak_rss_mb": 32.0,
+                "max_duration_s": 3.0,
+                "required_backpressure_tiers": ["Black"],
+            },
+        }
+        (root / "leak" / "run-1").mkdir(parents=True)
+        (root / "soak" / "run-1").mkdir(parents=True)
+        (root / "guards.json").write_text(json.dumps({"status": "passed"}), encoding="utf-8")
+        (root / "leak" / "run-1" / "summary.json").write_text(
+            json.dumps({"status": "passed"}), encoding="utf-8"
+        )
+        smoke_summary = root / "soak" / "run-1" / "smoke.json"
+        release_paths = [root / "soak" / "run-1" / f"release-{idx}.json" for idx in range(1, 4)]
+        sample_summary = {
+            "tests_run": 8,
+            "peak_rss_mb": 16.6,
+            "max_duration_s": 2.2,
+            "highest_backpressure_tier": "Black",
+            "pane_scales": [1, 50, 100, 200],
+            "metric_names": [f"metric-{idx}" for idx in range(8)],
+        }
+        smoke_summary.write_text(json.dumps(sample_summary), encoding="utf-8")
+        for path in release_paths:
+            path.write_text(json.dumps(sample_summary), encoding="utf-8")
+        (root / "soak" / "run-1" / "summary.json").write_text(
+            json.dumps({
+                "status": "passed",
+                "profiles": {
+                    "smoke": {"cycles": 1, "summary": smoke_summary.as_posix()},
+                    "release": {
+                        "cycles": 3,
+                        "summaries": [path.as_posix() for path in release_paths],
+                    },
+                },
+            }),
+            encoding="utf-8",
+        )
+        passing = evaluate_policy(root, root / "policy.json", copy.deepcopy(policy))
+        require(passing["status"] == "passed", "self_test_pass_case_failed", "expected passing case")
+
+        (root / "leak" / "run-1" / "summary.json").unlink()
+        failing = evaluate_policy(root, root / "policy.json", copy.deepcopy(policy))
+        require(failing["status"] == "failed", "self_test_fail_case_failed", "expected missing leak summary to fail")
+        leak_check = next(check for check in failing["checks"] if check["gate_id"] == "REL-01-leak-oracle")
+        require(
+            leak_check["reason_code"] == "leak_gate_missing_summary",
+            "self_test_wrong_reason",
+            "unexpected leak gate failure reason",
+            leak_check,
+        )
+
+
+repo_root = Path(sys.argv[1])
+policy_path = Path(sys.argv[2])
+out_path = Path(sys.argv[3])
+self_test = sys.argv[4] == "1"
+
+try:
+    require(policy_path.exists(), "policy_missing", f"policy missing at {policy_path}")
+    policy = load_json(policy_path)
+    if self_test:
+        run_self_tests()
+        out_path.write_text(
+            json.dumps(
+                {
+                    "checked_at": now_iso(),
+                    "status": "passed",
+                    "mode": "self-test",
+                    "policy_path": policy_path.as_posix(),
+                    "repo_root": repo_root.as_posix(),
+                },
+                indent=2,
+                sort_keys=True,
+            )
+            + "\n",
+            encoding="utf-8",
+        )
+        sys.exit(0)
+    report = evaluate_policy(repo_root, policy_path, policy)
+    out_path.write_text(json.dumps(report, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
+    sys.exit(0 if report["status"] == "passed" else 1)
+except GateFailure as exc:
+    report = {
+        "checked_at": now_iso(),
+        "status": "failed",
+        "error_code": exc.code,
+        "message": exc.message,
+        "detail": exc.detail,
+        "policy_path": policy_path.as_posix(),
+        "repo_root": repo_root.as_posix(),
+    }
+    out_path.write_text(json.dumps(report, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
+    sys.exit(1)
+except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover
+    report = {
+        "checked_at": now_iso(),
+        "status": "failed",
+        "error_code": "internal_error",
+        "message": str(exc),
+        "policy_path": policy_path.as_posix(),
+        "repo_root": repo_root.as_posix(),
+    }
+    out_path.write_text(json.dumps(report, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
+    sys.exit(2)
+PY

@@ -0,0 +1,247 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# E2E: validate ft-xbnl0.4.6 release-gate contract and diagnostics.
+set -euo pipefail
+
+ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)"
+BEAD_ID="ft-xbnl0.4.6"
+SCENARIO_ID="release_gates"
+RUN_ID="$(date -u +"%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ")"
+CORRELATION_ID="${BEAD_ID}-${RUN_ID}"
+ARTIFACT_DIR="${ROOT_DIR}/tests/e2e/artifacts/goal-line/${BEAD_ID}/${SCENARIO_ID}/${RUN_ID}"
+mkdir -p "${ARTIFACT_DIR}"
+
+COMMANDS_FILE="${ARTIFACT_DIR}/commands.txt"
+ENV_FILE="${ARTIFACT_DIR}/env.txt"
+STRUCTURED_LOG="${ARTIFACT_DIR}/structured.log"
+STDOUT_FILE="${ARTIFACT_DIR}/stdout.txt"
+STDERR_FILE="${ARTIFACT_DIR}/stderr.txt"
+SUMMARY_FILE="${ARTIFACT_DIR}/summary.json"
+
+exec > >(tee -a "${STDOUT_FILE}")
+exec 2> >(tee -a "${STDERR_FILE}" >&2)
+
+source "${ROOT_DIR}/tests/e2e/lib_rch_guards.sh"
+RCH_SKIP_SMOKE_PREFLIGHT=1
+rch_init "${ARTIFACT_DIR}" "${RUN_ID}" "ft_xbnl0_4_6_release_gates"
+
+PASS=0
+FAIL=0
+TOTAL=0
+REMOTE_TARGET_DIR="/tmp/ft-$(whoami)-target"
+
+record_command() {
+    printf '%s\n' "$*" >> "${COMMANDS_FILE}"
+}
+
+write_env() {
+    {
+        printf 'timestamp=%s\n' "$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")"
+        printf 'bead_id=%s\n' "${BEAD_ID}"
+        printf 'scenario_id=%s\n' "${SCENARIO_ID}"
+        printf 'correlation_id=%s\n' "${CORRELATION_ID}"
+        printf 'artifact_dir=%s\n' "${ARTIFACT_DIR}"
+        printf 'platform=%s\n' "$(uname -srm)"
+        printf 'cwd=%s\n' "${ROOT_DIR}"
+        printf 'remote_cargo_target_dir=%s\n' "${REMOTE_TARGET_DIR}"
+        printf 'rch_skip_smoke_preflight=%s\n' "${RCH_SKIP_SMOKE_PREFLIGHT}"
+    } > "${ENV_FILE}"
+}
+
+emit_log() {
+    local step="$1"
+    local status="$2"
+    local duration_ms="$3"
+    local message="$4"
+    jq -cn \
+        --arg timestamp "$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")" \
+        --arg bead_id "${BEAD_ID}" \
+        --arg scenario_id "${SCENARIO_ID}" \
+        --arg surface "release-gates" \
+        --arg step "${step}" \
+        --arg status "${status}" \
+        --arg correlation_id "${CORRELATION_ID}" \
+        --arg backend "rch" \
+        --arg platform "$(uname -srm)" \
+        --arg artifact_dir "${ARTIFACT_DIR}" \
+        --arg redaction "none" \
+        --arg message "${message}" \
+        --argjson duration_ms "${duration_ms}" \
+        '{
+          timestamp: $timestamp,
+          bead_id: $bead_id,
+          scenario_id: $scenario_id,
+          surface: $surface,
+          step: $step,
+          status: $status,
+          duration_ms: $duration_ms,
+          correlation_id: $correlation_id,
+          backend: $backend,
+          platform: $platform,
+          artifact_dir: $artifact_dir,
+          redaction: $redaction,
+          message: $message
+        }' >> "${STRUCTURED_LOG}"
+}
+
+record_result() {
+    local step="$1"
+    local ok="$2"
+    local duration_ms="$3"
+    local message="$4"
+    TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
+    if [[ "${ok}" == "true" ]]; then
+        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
+        emit_log "${step}" "passed" "${duration_ms}" "${message}"
+    else
+        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
+        emit_log "${step}" "failed" "${duration_ms}" "${message}"
+    fi
+}
+
+run_checked() {
+    local step="$1"
+    local log_file="$2"
+    shift 2
+    local start_ns end_ns duration_ms
+    start_ns="$(date +%s%N)"
+    record_command "$*"
+    if "$@" > "${log_file}" 2>&1; then
+        end_ns="$(date +%s%N)"
+        duration_ms="$(((end_ns - start_ns) / 1000000))"
+        record_result "${step}" "true" "${duration_ms}" "${log_file}"
+        return 0
+    fi
+    end_ns="$(date +%s%N)"
+    duration_ms="$(((end_ns - start_ns) / 1000000))"
+    record_result "${step}" "false" "${duration_ms}" "${log_file}"
+    return 1
+}
+
+run_rch_step() {
+    local step="$1"
+    local log_file="$2"
+    shift 2
+    local start_ns end_ns duration_ms
+    start_ns="$(date +%s%N)"
+    record_command "rch exec -- $*"
+    if run_rch_cargo_logged "${log_file}" "$@"; then
+        end_ns="$(date +%s%N)"
+        duration_ms="$(((end_ns - start_ns) / 1000000))"
+        record_result "${step}" "true" "${duration_ms}" "${log_file}"
+        return 0
+    fi
+    end_ns="$(date +%s%N)"
+    duration_ms="$(((end_ns - start_ns) / 1000000))"
+    record_result "${step}" "false" "${duration_ms}" "${log_file}"
+    return 1
+}
+
+echo "=== ${BEAD_ID} release gates ==="
+write_env
+command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1
+command -v rch >/dev/null 2>&1
+command -v rustfmt >/dev/null 2>&1
+record_command "ensure_rch_ready (RCH_SKIP_SMOKE_PREFLIGHT=${RCH_SKIP_SMOKE_PREFLIGHT})"
+ensure_rch_ready
+
+SOURCE_AUDIT_LOG="${ARTIFACT_DIR}/source_audit.log"
+run_checked \
+    "source_audit" \
+    "${SOURCE_AUDIT_LOG}" \
+    bash -lc "
+        set -euo pipefail
+        rg -n 'ft_xbnl0_4_6_release_gate_' '${ROOT_DIR}/crates/frankenterm-core/src/release_readiness_gates.rs'
+        test -f '${ROOT_DIR}/docs/ft-xbnl0-4-6-release-gates.json'
+        test -f '${ROOT_DIR}/scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_4_6_release_gates.sh'
+    "
+
+FMT_LOG="${ARTIFACT_DIR}/rustfmt_check.log"
+run_checked \
+    "rustfmt_check" \
+    "${FMT_LOG}" \
+    rustfmt --edition 2024 --check \
+        "${ROOT_DIR}/crates/frankenterm-core/src/release_readiness_gates.rs"
+
+SELF_TEST_LOG="${ARTIFACT_DIR}/validator_self_test.log"
+run_checked \
+    "validator_self_test" \
+    "${SELF_TEST_LOG}" \
+    bash "${ROOT_DIR}/scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_4_6_release_gates.sh" \
+        --self-test \
+        --output "${ARTIFACT_DIR}/release_gate_self_test.json"
+
+LIB_TEST_LOG="${ARTIFACT_DIR}/frankenterm_core_release_gate_tests.log"
+run_rch_step \
+    "frankenterm_core_release_gate_tests" \
+    "${LIB_TEST_LOG}" \
+    env CARGO_TARGET_DIR="${REMOTE_TARGET_DIR}" \
+        cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib ft_xbnl0_4_6_release_gate_ -- --nocapture
+rch_write_meta_json "${LIB_TEST_LOG}"
+
+VALIDATION_LOG="${ARTIFACT_DIR}/release_gate_repo_eval.log"
+record_command "bash ${ROOT_DIR}/scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_4_6_release_gates.sh --output ${ARTIFACT_DIR}/release_gate_repo_eval.json"
+set +e
+start_ns="$(date +%s%N)"
+bash "${ROOT_DIR}/scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_4_6_release_gates.sh" \
+    --output "${ARTIFACT_DIR}/release_gate_repo_eval.json" > "${VALIDATION_LOG}" 2>&1
+repo_eval_rc=$?
+set -e
+end_ns="$(date +%s%N)"
+duration_ms="$(((end_ns - start_ns) / 1000000))"
+if [[ ${repo_eval_rc} -eq 0 || ${repo_eval_rc} -eq 1 ]]; then
+    record_result "release_gate_repo_eval" "true" "${duration_ms}" "${VALIDATION_LOG}"
+else
+    record_result "release_gate_repo_eval" "false" "${duration_ms}" "${VALIDATION_LOG}"
+fi
+
+jq -cn \
+    --arg bead_id "${BEAD_ID}" \
+    --arg scenario_id "${SCENARIO_ID}" \
+    --arg correlation_id "${CORRELATION_ID}" \
+    --arg artifact_dir "${ARTIFACT_DIR}" \
+    --arg commands_file "${COMMANDS_FILE}" \
+    --arg env_file "${ENV_FILE}" \
+    --arg structured_log "${STRUCTURED_LOG}" \
+    --arg stdout_file "${STDOUT_FILE}" \
+    --arg stderr_file "${STDERR_FILE}" \
+    --arg source_audit_log "${SOURCE_AUDIT_LOG}" \
+    --arg fmt_log "${FMT_LOG}" \
+    --arg self_test_log "${SELF_TEST_LOG}" \
+    --arg lib_test_log "${LIB_TEST_LOG}" \
+    --arg lib_test_meta "$(rch_log_meta_path "${LIB_TEST_LOG}")" \
+    --arg repo_eval_log "${VALIDATION_LOG}" \
+    --arg repo_eval_json "${ARTIFACT_DIR}/release_gate_repo_eval.json" \
+    --argjson pass_count "${PASS}" \
+    --argjson fail_count "${FAIL}" \
+    --argjson total_count "${TOTAL}" \
+    '{
+      bead_id: $bead_id,
+      scenario_id: $scenario_id,
+      status: (if $fail_count == 0 then "passed" else "failed" end),
+      correlation_id: $correlation_id,
+      artifact_dir: $artifact_dir,
+      pass_count: $pass_count,
+      fail_count: $fail_count,
+      total_count: $total_count,
+      artifacts: {
+        commands: $commands_file,
+        env: $env_file,
+        structured_log: $structured_log,
+        stdout: $stdout_file,
+        stderr: $stderr_file,
+        source_audit: $source_audit_log,
+        rustfmt_check: $fmt_log,
+        validator_self_test: $self_test_log,
+        frankenterm_core_release_gate_tests: $lib_test_log,
+        frankenterm_core_release_gate_tests_meta: $lib_test_meta,
+        release_gate_repo_eval: $repo_eval_log,
+        release_gate_repo_eval_json: $repo_eval_json
+      }
+    }' > "${SUMMARY_FILE}"
+
+if [[ "${FAIL}" -ne 0 ]]; then
+    echo "ft-xbnl0.4.6 release-gate verification FAILED. Summary: ${SUMMARY_FILE}" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+echo "ft-xbnl0.4.6 release-gate verification passed. Summary: ${SUMMARY_FILE}"
